page: Microsoft Azure E64s_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Esv6 server family. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz, with 32 physical cores and hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

In benchmark testing, the E64s_v6 demonstrates strong multi-core and memory performance. The server achieved a top-tier rating in large-block memory bandwidth, with read-only speeds of 430.32 GB/sec and write speeds of 185.29 GB/sec. Multi-core processing is also robust, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 20,795.0 and a Passmark CPU physics test score of 14,254.22, both landing in the top tier. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core scoring in the average tier.

Given its high memory density and strong multi-threaded capabilities, the E64s_v6 is suited for memory-intensive workloads such as database operations, where it achieved a top-tier Passmark database score of 36,080.41. It also performs well in static web serving, yielding 91.27 GB/sec throughput, and handles medium-sized LLM inference tasks efficiently. The absence of local storage means users must provision external Premium Storage, which represents a qualitative cost tradeoff for workloads requiring high-speed local scratch space.
description: Microsoft Azure E64s_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server from the Esv6 family, featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz across 32 physical cores, this server delivers strong multi-threaded performance, highlighted by a top-tier Geekbench multi-core score of 20,795.0. It lacks local storage and GPUs but is Premium Storage capable. Benchmark results show exceptional large-block memory bandwidth, with read speeds reaching 430.32 GB/sec, and top-tier database operations performance. These characteristics make the E64s_v6 highly effective for memory-intensive enterprise applications, relational databases, and large-scale web serving. However, the lack of bundled local storage requires external storage provisioning, which is a key architectural tradeoff to consider for data-heavy deployments.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64s_v6 is a memory-optimized server with 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 512 GB RAM, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and database performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64s_v6 features 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and top-tier memory bandwidth, optimized for memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized platform featuring sixty-four dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and top-tier memory bandwidth for data-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C vCPUs running at 4.2 GHz
512.0 GB of RAM providing 8.0 GB per core memory density
Top-tier large-block memory read bandwidth of 430.32 GB/sec
No local storage, requiring external Premium Storage provisioning
Optimized for relational databases, web serving, and memory-intensive applications
